hero.name: Keegan Tran
hero.tagline:
CS & Financial Economics @ University of Washington
Building pipelines, ML systems, and data-driven applications that turn complexity into clarity.

about.headline:
Building at the
intersection of
data & software.
about.bio1: I'm Keegan, a Computer Science and Financial Economics student at the University of Washington graduating June 2026. I combine rigorous quantitative thinking with hands-on engineering from NLP research pipelines to hardware validation automation.
about.bio2: My work spans the full stack: Python automation, machine learning models, distributed databases, and interactive data visualizations. I care deeply about the "why" when programming systems that are measurably better, not just technically correct.

experience.title: Test Engineer Intern
bullets: Developed Python automation tools that parse In-Circuit Testing machine logs into structured datasets, converting raw measurements into analyzable CSV outputs.
Implemented configurable test selection, multi-measurement parsing, and automated failure alerts.
Solution adopted by 15+ engineers to analyze 100+ PCB test runs weekly, improving debugging speed and reliability of hardware validation workflows.

experience.title: NLP Research Assistant
bullets: Built an NLP pipeline using BERTopic to cluster 100k+ National Cancer Institute grant abstracts, enabling large-scale topic discovery for research prioritization.
Applied Elbow Method and clustering evaluation to determine optimal topic granularity.
Visualized funding allocation patterns using Altair, identifying spending trends across research domains.

experience.title: Learning Technologies Consultant Lead
bullets: Led training and onboarding for 20+ technical consultants, redesigning technical curricula and mock ticket systems to improve troubleshooting readiness.
Delivered Tier-2 support for enterprise platforms (Canvas, Zoom, Panopto) to thousands of UW stakeholders.
Resolved software bugs, documented solutions, and collaborated with vendors to improve service reliability.

experience.title: AI Intern
bullets: Developed a sales forecasting web application for a supermarket chain using machine learning models with rolling averages, time-lag features, and historical sales data.
Performed feature engineering, dimensionality reduction, and data cleaning, improving model prediction accuracy by 17%.

projects.title: Interactive Efficient Frontier Portfolio Visualizer
projects.summary: An interactive tool explaining Modern Portfolio Theory, letting users explore risk/return tradeoffs through live parameter controls.
projects.problem: Modern Portfolio Theory is mathematically dense and hard to intuit. Most explanations are static where learners can't feel how changing one variable shifts the entire frontier.
projects.solution: Built a D3.js-powered visualization where users drag sliders to adjust asset weights, correlations, and the risk-free rate. The frontier redraws in real-time, making abstract math visceral.
projects.why: I wanted to make quantitative finance accessible: the kind of tool I wish I'd had in my Financial Economics classes.
results: Real-time Sharpe ratio & volatility recalculation
Dynamic Capital Market Line rendering
Fully interactive asset weight configuration

projects.title: Distributed Flight Reservation System
projects.summary: A Java backend flight booking system with Azure SQL integration, handling concurrent reservations safely at scale.
projects.problem: Flight booking systems handle thousands of concurrent transactions. Without proper locking, double-bookings and race conditions corrupt data and break user trust.
projects.solution: Implemented ACID-compliant transactions with two-phase locking and deadlock detection on top of Azure SQL. The system supports 1M+ flight records with guaranteed consistency under high concurrency.
projects.why: CSE344 taught me that databases are trust infrastructure. I engineered this system to meet that standard under real concurrency pressure.
results: Handled 1M+ flight records via Azure SQL
Zero data corruption under concurrent load
Two-phase locking with automatic deadlock resolution
Full ACID transaction guarantees

projects.title: Computer Vision Waste Classification System
projects.summary: A ResNet-based model that classifies waste images into compost, recycling, and landfill categories, deployed as a live web app.
projects.problem: Contamination in recycling bins costs municipalities millions annually. Most people don't know how to sort waste correctly, and manual sorting is slow and error-prone.
projects.solution: Fine-tuned a ResNet architecture on 2,000+ labeled waste images. Built a Flask web app where users upload a photo and receive instant classification with confidence scores.
projects.why: I wanted to demonstrate that computer vision can be applied to real sustainability problems and not just academic benchmarks.
results: Trained on 2,000+ images across 3 waste categories
Real-time image classification via Flask API
Deployed as a live web application

projects.title: AstraPath: Supply Chain Route Optimizer
projects.summary: A graph-based supply chain application that lets logistics teams model distribution networks and find optimal routes by cost, speed, distance, or emissions.
projects.problem: Real-world supply chains are graphs: factories, warehouses, and endpoints connected by routes, but most tools model them as flat spreadsheets, making multi-hop pathfinding slow, manual, and inaccurate.
projects.solution: Built AstraPath on Neo4j AuraDB, a cloud-hosted graph database purpose-built for relationship traversal. Users define their network visually, specify transport modes (truck, plane, train, EV), and CO2 emissions and travel time are auto-calculated. Cypher queries with reduce() over variable-length relationships power the pathfinding engine, while Neovis.js renders the live network graph.
projects.why: I wanted to explore graph databases beyond relational SQL for a problem where relationships ARE the data. Supply chain optimization is a perfect fit: the path IS the answer.
results: 4 optimization criteria: distance, cost, emissions, and travel time
Auto-calculated CO2 and time based on transport mode
Interactive network graph via Neovis.js
Cypher-powered variable-length pathfinding on Neo4j AuraDB

projects.title: Duet: Music-Based Dating App
projects.summary: A dating app that matches users by music taste using real Spotify listening data, with swipe-based discovery and real-time messaging.
projects.problem: Most dating apps rely on photos and demographics, but music taste is one of the most predictive signals of personality and compatibility. There was no app that surfaced genuine listening data as the primary matching signal.
projects.solution: Integrated Spotify OAuth to pull each user's top 10 tracks and rank potential matches by track overlap. The more shared listening history, the higher the match score. Built a swipe feed showing name, location, bio, and top 5 tracks with album art. When both users like each other, a Firestore conversation is created instantly. Real-time messaging syncs across both devices with no refresh required.
projects.why: A team of 6 developers and I wanted to build a social product where the data actually meant something, where the algorithm reflected real taste rather than surface-level profile signals.
results: Spotify OAuth integration with live top-track data
Match ranking by track overlap intersection algorithm
Real-time messaging via Firestore
4-stage account creation wizard with Firebase Auth
Collaborated across 6 developers using Git branching and PRs

projects.title: Mini Google: Full-Stack Search Engine in C/C++
projects.summary: A full-stack search engine built entirely from scratch in C and C++, from custom data structures to a browser-accessible web interface. Type a query in your browser and ranked results come back in milliseconds — served dynamically from disk.
projects.problem: Modern search engines abstract away everything: data structures, disk I/O, indexing, HTTP, and concurrency. Understanding how they actually work means building each layer yourself, with no framework to catch mistakes.
solution: Data structures (C): Built LinkedList and HashTable from scratch with iterators and destructors — the foundation every other module depends on.

In-memory index (C): FileParser extracts word positions from documents; CrawlFileTree walks directories; DocTable maps file paths to numeric doc IDs; MemIndex is a full inverted index (word → doc ID → offsets list).
Disk-based index (C++): WriteIndex serializes DocTable and MemIndex to disk; IndexTableReader reconstructs them with byte-offset parsing, memory mapping, and endianness handling.

Web server (C++): Thread pool handles concurrent TCP connections; QueryProcessor ranks multi-term queries from disk indices; HTTP layer routes dynamic search results and static files with XSS escaping and path sanitization.
projects.why: CSE333 is the course that made systems programming click. Every abstraction I'd taken for granted — hash maps, search indices, web servers, memory safety — I had to build and debug myself. Valgrind found the leaks. The browser found the bugs.
results: Custom LinkedList and HashTable in C, used end-to-end across all modules
In-memory inverted index with word-level positional data and ranked query results
Disk-based index with byte-offset management, endianness handling, and memory mapping
Multi-threaded HTTP/1.1 server with thread pool serving live search from browser
XSS escaping and directory traversal prevention for real security hardening
Verified memory-safe throughout with Valgrind

projects.title: Yoga by GiGi: Instructor Portfolio Website & CMS
projects.summary: A Next.js website for a yoga instructor with a Contentful-powered CMS, letting the client manage blogs, retreats, and pages without touching code.
projects.problem: Yoga instructors need to publish blog posts, announce retreats, and update pages regularly, but relying on a developer for every content change is slow and expensive. Static sites with hardcoded content don't scale for real clients.
projects.solution: Built on Next.js with Contentful as a headless CMS. Defined custom content types (Blog, Retreat, CustomPage) so Gigi can create and edit content through Contentful's UI. A dynamic page builder renders fully configurable sections: headings, text, images, CTAs, video embeds from Contentful data alone. All pages use ISR with 60-second revalidation which are fast like static, yet also fresh like dynamic. Google Maps API renders retreat locations geographically.
projects.why: I wanted to build something a real client would use every week. That means the CMS has to be intuitive enough for a non-developer to operate independently.
results: Fully client-managed content via Contentful CMS
Dynamic page builder: no code changes needed to add sections
ISR with 60s revalidation for performance and freshness
Google Maps integration for retreat geolocation
Delivered documentation to client for content onboarding

contact.headline: Let's build something...
contact.subtext: I'm actively seeking full-time roles in software or data engineering for June 2026. If you have an opportunity, project idea, or just want to connect, feel free to reach out!
